title: rhodiola rosea var. scopolii
intro: Rhodiola rosea var. scopolii, also known as the Arctic rose or Scopolia rosea, is a plant species that belongs to the Rhodiola genus and is native to the mountainous regions of the Arctic tundra. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as rosmarinic acid and tyrosol. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea var. scopolii include improved mental clarity, reduced stress and fatigue, enhanced mood, and improved sleep quality, making it a popular herbal remedy for individuals dealing with anxiety and depression. Rhodiola rosea var. scopolii is commonly consumed as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas, and can also be found in herbal preparations such as tinctures and extracts.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", rhodiola rosea var. scopolii has been shown to have beneficial effects on reversing or healing cardiovascular and reproductive health by addressing non-specific stress damage.
